preview_text:
The model favors Croatia decisively, assigning a 79% win probability compared to Ghana's 2.6%. This represents a wide gulf in expected performance. The model sees Ghana as a substantial underdog with minimal realistic path to victory.

The match appears highly asymmetric in the model's forecast. Croatia is heavily favored across all three outcomes, with a draw at 18% offering the only real alternative to a Croatia win. A Ghana victory registers as a low-probability tail event. The gap between the favorite and the field reflects a clear expectation of Croatian dominance.

A sharp divergence exists between the model and sportsbook consensus. The books assign Croatia only 57% win probability—22 percentage points below the model—while pricing Ghana at 18%, roughly seven times higher than the model's 2.6% estimate. Polymarket similarly shows 59% for Croatia and 16% for Ghana. The dashboard does not adjudicate which assessment better reflects true match uncertainty; the disagreement is material and worth noting.
